title: DFW Policy Template Schema
description: Validates DFW policy-as-code YAML/JSON definitions for NSX Distributed Firewall rules. Used by PolicyDeployer.js to validate policy structure before deploying to NSX-T, and by the CI pipeline (validate-policies) to enforce policy correctness at build time.

policy_name.description: Unique policy identifier (alphanumeric, hyphens, underscores). Used as the NSX-T policy ID.

display_name.description: Human-readable policy name displayed in the NSX Manager UI.

nsx_category.description: NSX DFW policy category. Determines processing order: Emergency > Infrastructure > Environment > Application.

scope.description: Policy scope. GLOBAL applies to both sites via NSX Federation. LOCAL:{site} applies only to the specified site's NSX Manager.

priority.description: Policy priority within its category. Lower values = higher precedence. Emergency policies should use 1-100, Infrastructure 101-1000, Environment 1001-5000, Application 5001-10000.

description.description: Optional policy description. Included in NSX-T policy metadata for documentation.
examples: Three-tier application firewall rules for APP001 (Web -> Application -> Database)

tags.description: Optional NSX-T tags applied to the policy itself for organizational purposes.

applied_to.description: Groups to which this policy is applied. If omitted, the policy applies based on rule-level source/destination groups. Use for policy scoping to limit DFW rule evaluation overhead.

locked.description: Whether the policy is locked from manual edits in the NSX Manager UI. Automation-managed policies should be locked to prevent drift.

rules.description: Ordered list of DFW rules within this policy. Rules are evaluated top-to-bottom within the policy.

name.description: Unique rule identifier within the policy. Used as the NSX-T rule ID.

display_name.description: Human-readable rule name displayed in the NSX Manager UI.

items.description: NSX-T group path, IP set path, or 'ANY' for all sources.

source_groups.description: Source security groups, IP sets, or 'ANY'. References NSX-T group paths.

items.description: NSX-T group path, IP set path, or 'ANY' for all destinations.

destination_groups.description: Destination security groups, IP sets, or 'ANY'. References NSX-T group paths.

items.description: Service definition: protocol/port, port range, NSX-T service path, or 'ANY'.

services.description: Service definitions. Supports protocol/port (TCP/443), port ranges (TCP/8080-8090), named services (/infra/services/...), ICMP, or ANY.

action.description: Rule action. ALLOW permits traffic, DROP silently discards, REJECT discards with ICMP unreachable.

direction.description: Traffic direction. IN = ingress to destination, OUT = egress from source, IN_OUT = bidirectional.

ip_protocol.description: IP protocol version filter for the rule.

logged.description: Whether rule hits are logged to the syslog server. Enable for audit-sensitive rules (compliance, quarantine).

disabled.description: Whether the rule is disabled. Disabled rules remain in the policy but are not enforced. Useful for staging rules before activation.

sequence_number.description: Explicit rule ordering within the policy. Lower values are evaluated first. If omitted, rules are ordered by array position.

tag.description: Optional rule-level tag for grouping and filtering in NSX Manager.

notes.description: Documentation notes for the rule. Included in policy audit reports.
examples: Allows HTTPS traffic from web tier to application tier per FR-SEC-001

scope.description: Rule-level scope to further restrict which groups this rule applies to, beyond the policy-level applied_to.
